Source   Edit  

Types

AnyPointer = (when defined(js):
  JsRoot
 else:
  pointer)
Source   Edit   
Dictionary = object
  data*: seq[LoadReader]
Source   Edit   
DictionaryId = distinct DictionaryIdImpl
Source   Edit   
DictionaryIdImpl = int
Source   Edit   
GrimeDictError = object of GrimeError
error for a value that could not be found in the dictionary Source   Edit   
GrimeDumper = object
  dict*: FlushWriter
  dictIds*: OrderedTable[ReferenceIdentity, DictionaryId]
  data*: FlushWriter
Source   Edit   
GrimeDumpFormat = object
  shared*: GrimeFormat
Source   Edit   
GrimeError = object of ValueError
Source   Edit   
GrimeFormat = object
  endian*: Endianness = littleEndian
  dict*: bool
Source   Edit   
GrimeReader = object
  dict*: Dictionary
  dictPointers*: OrderedTable[DictionaryId, AnyPointer]
  data*: LoadReader
Source   Edit   
GrimeReadError = object of GrimeError
error for invalid binary Source   Edit   
GrimeReadFormat = object
  shared*: GrimeFormat
  skip*: bool                ## skip current value
Source   Edit   
GrimeValueError = object of GrimeError
error for when a value can be read, but could not be fit into the expected value Source   Edit   
ReferenceIdentity = distinct uint
Source   Edit   
SomeGrimeFormat = GrimeReadFormat | GrimeDumpFormat
Source   Edit  

Consts

grimeTrackJsDictReferences {.booldefine.} = true
uses a global map to get the reference identities of reference objects in JS to use in dicts Source   Edit  

Procs

proc `==`(a, b: DictionaryId): bool {.borrow, ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Source   Edit   
proc `==`(a, b: ReferenceIdentity): bool {.borrow, ...raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Source   Edit   
proc hash(a: DictionaryId): Hash {.borrow, ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Source   Edit   
proc hash(a: ReferenceIdentity): Hash {.borrow, ...raises: [], tags: [],
                                        forbids: [].}
Source   Edit
